seoTitle: How to Host a Next.js Website on Hostinger (2026)
metaDescription: Deploy a Next.js app on Hostinger with this walkthrough covering hosting setup, file structure, environment variables, and going live.
intro: Deploying a Next.js app on Hostinger involves more than just uploading files — you need the right hosting setup, correct file structure, and properly configured environment variables. This guide walks through the full process using Hostinger's file manager or FTP, whether your project uses SSR, SSG, or a static export. By the end, you'll have your Next.js site live on affordable, fast hosting.

steps.heading: Set up your Hostinger hosting environment
steps.body: Start by preparing the Hostinger hosting plan you'll deploy to, since the right plan and setup depend on whether your Next.js app uses SSR, SSG, or static export.

steps.heading: Configure the file structure
steps.body: Organize your project's build output correctly so Hostinger can serve the right files, which is a key step before uploading anything.

steps.heading: Upload your build
steps.body: Use Hostinger's file manager or FTP to upload your Next.js build files to the hosting environment.

steps.heading: Set environment variables
steps.body: Configure any environment variables your Next.js app relies on so it runs correctly once deployed on Hostinger.

steps.heading: Preview the live site
steps.body: After deployment, load your site to confirm everything works as expected in the live environment.

faq.question: Can I host a Next.js app on Hostinger shared hosting?
faq.answer: Yes, this depends on how your project is built — static exports work well on shared hosting, while SSR setups may need additional configuration.

faq.question: Do I need FTP to deploy Next.js on Hostinger?
faq.answer: You can use either Hostinger's file manager or FTP to upload your build, depending on what's more convenient for you.

faq.question: What's the difference between deploying SSR vs static Next.js sites?
faq.answer: SSR apps require a running server environment, while static exports can be uploaded as plain files, which affects how you configure hosting.

faq.question: Why do I need to set environment variables after deployment?
faq.answer: Next.js apps often rely on environment variables for configuration, and these need to be set correctly on Hostinger for the app to function properly.

faq.question: Is Hostinger a good option for Next.js hosting?
faq.answer: It's presented here as an affordable and fast hosting option suitable for deploying Next.js projects of different types.
